title: Adapter Capability Declaration
description: Describes what an adapter can produce, what fields it adds to result packets, which redaction rules it applies, and its known limitations. Every adapter in fixtures/adapters/capabilities/ must conform to this schema.

adapter_id.description: Stable identifier for the adapter runtime, e.g. 'hermes', 'cli', 'human-baseline'.

adapter_type.description: Broad category: agent-runtime (OpenClaw), orchestrator (Hermes), automation (CLI scripts), manual (human operator).

display_name.description: Human-readable name for dashboards and reports.

description.description: Short paragraph describing what this adapter does and when to use it.

standard_statuses.description: Which of the five standard statuses this adapter can produce.

internal_mapping.description: Maps internal state names to standard statuses.

status.description: Status mapping: internal adapter states mapped to standard result packet statuses.

evidence_kinds.description: Which evidence kinds this adapter commonly produces.

runtime_fields.description: Adapter-specific optional fields the adapter may add to result packets. Keys are field names, values are one-sentence descriptions.

redaction_rules.description: Redaction rules this adapter supports, matching the contract redaction taxonomy.

approval_boundaries.description: Describes what actions this adapter considers approval-sensitive.

timeout_behavior.description: Describes how this adapter handles time limits.

task_families.description: Which event families this adapter is designed for.

fixture_dependencies.description: Describes what fixture data the adapter typically needs.

known_limitations.description: Documented limitations or gaps in this adapter's implementation.
